Clean Code by Robert C. Martin

This book covers the principles and practices of writing clean code.
Clean code reads like well-written prose. It is focused, expressive, and minimal.

The author presents numerous examples showing how to transform messy code
into clean code through refactoring. Each chapter covers a different aspect
of code quality including naming, functions, comments, formatting, objects,
error handling, and unit tests.

The book argues that writing clean code is both an art and a craft that
requires discipline and continuous practice. The rules presented are not
absolute but provide a framework for thinking about code quality.
